_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Behavioral and Emotional Disorders of Childhood and Adolescence
procedure_name: Other Internalizing and Externalizing Disorders of Childhood and Adolescence
source_files: Behavioral & Emotional Disorders of Childhood and Adolescence_Medco.pdf
Behavioral & Emotional Disorders of Childhood and Adolescence_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Internalizing disorders are those characterized by anxiety, depressive, and somatic symptoms and externalizing disorders are those characterized by impulsive, disruptive conduct, substance use, and other addictive symptoms.
The characteristics beyond these internalizing and externalizing disorders will fall in this category.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailed history and chronicity
Admission document signed by empanelled psychiatrist

ppd_preauth.q: Was patient admission document signed by an empanelled psychiatrist?

common_queries: Admission document not signed by an empanelled psychiatrist.
Clinical notes lack detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment, or justification for hospitalization.
Discharge summary does not mention a date of follow-up.
Treating doctor does not meet the minimum qualification (MD/DNB/equivalent Psychiatry).
The disorder is not associated with physiological disturbances or physical factors as per the disclaimer.

additional_information.special_empanelment: As per the provisions of the Mental Health Act 2017
clinical_key_pointers: The provisions under Mental Healthcare Act 2017 (https://egazette.nic.in/WriteReadData/2017/175248.pdf) be referred for details on Admission & Discharge criteria.
Neurodevelopmental disorders other than intellectual disorders come under ICD 11 and DSM-5.
